- `"default"` (default) - Inherits the setting from the `questionErrorLocation` property specified for the survey.
- `"top"` - Displays error messages above questions.
- `"bottom"` - Displays error messages below questions.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/set-properties-on-multiple-questions-using-panel/ (linkStyle))

name: PanelModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Panel container element.

A panel can contain questions and other panels. Refer to the following help topic for an illustration: [Survey Structure](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey-create-a-simple-survey#survey-structure).

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-panel/ (linkStyle))

members.name: page
members.documentation: Returns a page to which the panel belongs and allows you to move this panel to a different page.

members.name: visibleIndex
members.documentation:
Returns the visible index of the panel in the survey. Commonly it is -1 and it doesn't show.
You have to set showNumber to true to show index/numbering for the Panel

members.name: showNumber
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to show the panel number in the title.

Default value: `false`

members.name: showQuestionNumbers
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to display survey element numbers within this page/panel and how to calculate them.

Possible values:

- `"default"` - Inherits the setting from the parent panel, page, or survey.
- `"recursive"` - Applies recursive numbering to elements nested within this page/panel (for example, 1 -> 1.1 -> 1.1.1, etc.).
- `"onpanel"` - Starts numbering within this page/panel from scratch.
- `"off"` - Hides question numbers within this page/panel.

members.name: no
members.documentation:
A question number or letter (depends on the `questionStartIndex` property).

When the question number, title, or the entire question is invisible, this property returns an empty string.

members.name: innerIndent
members.documentation: Increases or decreases an indent of panel content from the left edge. Accepts positive integer values and 0.

members.name: startWithNewLine
members.documentation:
Disable this property if you want to render the current panel on the same line or row with the previous question or panel.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/arrange-multiple-questions-in-single-line/ (linkStyle))

members.name: expand
members.documentation: Expands the panel.

parameters.name: focusFirstQuestion
parameters.documentation: Specifies whether to focus the first question within the expanded panel. Default value: `true`.

name: PageModel
documentation:
The `PageModel` object describes a survey page and contains properties and methods that allow you to control the page and access its elements (panels and questions).

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/nps-question/ (linkStyle))

members.name: navigationTitle
members.documentation:
A caption displayed on a navigation button in the TOC or progress bar. Applies when [`showTOC`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#showTOC) is `true` or when the [progress bar is visible](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#showProgressBar), [`progressBarType`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/surveymodel#progressBarType) is set to `"pages"`, and [`progressBarShowPageTitles`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/surveymodel#progressBarShowPageTitles) is `true`.

[Table of Contents Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/table-of-contents/ (linkStyle))

[Progress Bar Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/configure-form-navigation-with-progress-indicators/ (linkStyle))

> If navigation titles are unspecified, the navigation buttons display page [titles](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/page-model#title) or [names](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/pagemodel#name).

members.name: visibleIndex
members.documentation: The visible index of the page. It has values from 0 to visible page count - 1.

members.name: isStartPage
members.documentation:
Returns `true` if this page is a start page.

Refer to the following help topic for more information on how to configure a start page: [Start Page](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey-create-a-multi-page-survey#start-page).

members.name: showNavigationButtons
members.documentation:
Gets or sets the visibility of the Start, Next, Previous, and Complete navigation buttons on this page. Overrides the [`showNavigationButtons`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#showNavigationButtons) property specified on the survey-level.

Default value: `undefined` (the visibility depends on the survey-level setting)

members.name: isActive
members.documentation: Returns `true` if this is the current page.

members.name: wasShown
members.documentation: Returns `true` if the respondent has already seen this page during the current session.

members.name: scrollToTop
members.documentation: Scrolls this page to the top.

members.name: timeSpent
members.documentation: A time period that a respondent has spent on this page so far; measured in seconds. Applies only to [quiz surveys](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey-create-a-quiz).

members.name: getAllPanels
members.documentation: Returns a list of all panels on this page.

parameters.name: visibleOnly
parameters.documentation: A Boolean value that specifies whether to include only visible panels.

parameters.name: includingDesignTime
parameters.documentation: For internal use.

members.name: timeLimit
members.documentation:
A time period that a respondent has to complete this page; measured in seconds. Applies only to [quiz surveys](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey-create-a-quiz).

Default value: 0 (time is unlimited)

Alternatively, you can use the `SurveyModel`'s [`timeLimitPerPage`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/surveymodel#timeLimitPerPage) property to specify identical time periods for all survey pages.

parameters.name: focusFirstQuestion
parameters.documentation: Specifies whether to focus the first question within the expanded panel. Default value: `true`.

members.name: question
members.documentation: A Question instance for which the event is raised.

members.name: question
members.documentation: A File Upload or Signature Pad question instance for which the event is raised.

members.name: question
members.documentation: A Dynamic Panel question instance for which the event is raised.

members.name: question
members.documentation: A Multi-Select Matrix question instance for which the event is raised.

members.name: question
members.documentation: A Dynamic Matrix question instance for which the event is raised.

members.name: panel
members.documentation: A Panel instance for which the event is raised.

members.name: page
members.documentation: A Page instance for which the event is raised.

members.name: actions
members.documentation: An array of [actions](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/iaction) associated with the processed element.

members.name: actions
members.documentation: An array of [actions](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/iaction). You can modify the entire array or individual actions within it.

members.name: htmlElement
members.documentation: A rendered HTML element.

members.name: cssClasses
members.documentation: An object with CSS classes applied to the element being rendered, for example, `{ root: "class1", button: "class2" }`. You can modify this object to apply custom CSS classes.

members.name: visible
members.documentation: Indicates whether the element is visible now.

members.name: trigger
members.documentation: A trigger that has been executed.

members.name: isCompleteOnTrigger
members.documentation: Returns `true` if survey completion is caused by a ["complete" trigger](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey/conditional-logic#complete).

members.name: completeTrigger
members.documentation: A "complete" trigger that has been executed. This parameter has a value only if `options.isCompleteOnTrigger` is `true`.

members.name: allow
members.documentation: A Boolean property that you can set to `false` if you want to prevent survey completion.

members.name: message
members.documentation: A notification message to display when survey results cannot be saved or after they have been saved successfully.

members.name: clearSaveMessages
members.documentation: Call this method to hide the save operation messages.

members.name: showSaveSuccess
members.documentation: Call this method to indicate that survey results are successfully saved. You can use the `text` parameter to display a custom message.

members.name: showSaveError
members.documentation: Call this method to indicate that an error occurred during the save operation. You can use the `text` parameter to display a custom error message.

members.name: showSaveInProgress
members.documentation: Call this method to indicate that the save operation is in progress. You can use the `text` parameter to display a custom message.

members.name: allow
members.documentation: A Boolean property that you can set to `false` if you want to cancel the preview.

members.name: allow
members.documentation: A Boolean property that you can set to `false` if you want to cancel the navigation and show the [complete page](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey/create-a-multi-page-survey#complete-page).

members.name: url
members.documentation: A URL to which respondents should be navigated. You can modify this parameter's value.

members.name: isPrevPage
members.documentation: Returns `true` if the respondent is switching to the previous page.

members.name: isNextPage
members.documentation: Returns `true` if the respondent is switching to the next page.

members.name: isGoingBackward
members.documentation: Returns `true` if the respondent is going backward, that is, `newCurrentPage` or `newCurrentQuestion` is earlier in the survey than `oldCurrentPage`  or `oldCurrentQuestion`.

members.name: isGoingForward
members.documentation: Returns `true` if the respondent is going forward along the survey.

members.name: isAfterPreview
members.documentation: Returns `true` if the respondent is switching from the [preview page](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey/create-a-multi-page-survey#preview-page).

members.name: newCurrentPage
members.documentation: The current page.

members.name: oldCurrentPage
members.documentation:
A page that used to be current.\
In [question-per-page mode](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#questionsOnPageMode), the `oldCurrentPage` and `newCurrentPage` parameters may contain the same `PageModel` instance. This is because the survey doesn't create artificial pages to display only one question per page. If both the previous and current questions belong to the same page in the survey JSON schema, they have the same parent `PageModel` instance.

members.name: oldCurrentQuestion
members.documentation:
The current question.\
This parameter has a value only in [question-per-page mode](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#questionsOnPageMode).

members.name: newCurrentQuestion
members.documentation:
A question that used to be current.\
This parameter has a value only in [question-per-page mode](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#questionsOnPageMode).

members.name: allow
members.documentation: A Boolean property that you can set to `false` if you do not want to switch the current page.

members.name: message
members.documentation: A notification message to display when the page cannot be switched.

members.name: name
members.documentation: The `name` of the question whose value is being changed. If you use the [`valueName`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/text-entry-question-model#valueName) property, this parameter contains its value.

members.name: reason
members.documentation: A value that indicates what caused the value change: an [expression](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey/conditional-logic#expressions) evaluation or a run of a [trigger](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey/conditional-logic#conditional-survey-logic-triggers). If the value is changed for other reasons, this parameter is `undefined`.

members.name: value
members.documentation: A new value.

members.name: value
members.documentation: A new value. You can change it if required.

members.name: oldValue
members.documentation: A previous value.

members.name: value
members.documentation: A new value for the variable or calculated value.

members.name: name
members.documentation: The name of the variable or calculated value that has been changed.

members.name: name
members.documentation: The question's name.

members.name: page
members.documentation: A page that nests the added element.

members.name: parent
members.documentation: The parent container (panel or page).

members.name: index
members.documentation: The element's index within the parent container (panel or page).

members.name: name
members.documentation: The question's name.

members.name: name
members.documentation: The element's name.

members.name: validator
members.documentation: A validator instance for which the event is raised.

members.name: pattern
members.documentation: A regular expression pattern used by the validator. You can modify this value to change the validation logic.

members.name: flags
members.documentation: Optional regular expression flags that control additional matching behavior, such as case-insensitive searching. You can modify this value. For more information about supported flags, refer to the MDN article: [Advanced searching with flags](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Guide/Regular_expressions#advanced_searching_with_flags).

members.name: value
members.documentation: A question value being validated.

members.name: name
members.documentation: The question's name.

members.name: error
members.documentation: An error message that you should specify if custom validation fails.

members.name: errors
members.documentation: An array of other validation errors that you can modify. The array is empty if the validated question satisfies all validation rules.

members.name: errors
members.documentation: An array of errors. The array is empty if the validated question satisfies all validation rules.

members.name: complete
members.documentation: A method that you should call when a request to the server has completed.

members.name: errors
members.documentation: An object for your error messages. Set error messages as follows: `options.errors["questionName"] = "My error message"`.

members.name: data
members.documentation: Question values. You can get an individual question value as follows: `options.data["questionName"]`.

members.name: name
members.documentation: The panel's name.

members.name: error
members.documentation: An error message that you should specify if custom validation fails.

members.name: errors
members.documentation: An array of other validation errors that you can modify.

members.name: name
members.documentation: A validation error type: `"required"`, `"requireoneanswer"`, `"requirenumeric"`, `"exceedsize"`, `"webrequest"`, `"webrequestempty"`, `"otherempty"`, `"uploadingfile"`, `"requiredinallrowserror"`, `"minrowcounterror"`, `"keyduplicationerror"`, or `"custom"`.

members.name: obj
members.documentation: A survey element to which the validation error belongs.

members.name: error
members.documentation: A validation error.

members.name: text
members.documentation: An error message. You can assign a custom message to this parameter.

members.name: questions
members.documentation: An array of questions with validation errors.

members.name: errors
members.documentation: An array of validation errors.

members.name: html
members.documentation: HTML markup. You can modify this parameter's value.

members.name: reason
members.documentation: Indicates a page, question, or message for which HTML content is intended: [`"completed"`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#completedHtml) | [`"completed-before"`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#completedBeforeHtml) | [`"loading"`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#loadingHtml) | [`"html-question"`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/add-custom-html-to-survey#html).

members.name: title
members.documentation: A question title taken from the question's `title` or `name` property. You can change this parameter's value.

members.name: element
members.documentation: A survey element (question, panel, page, or the survey itself) for which the event is raised.

members.name: tagName
members.documentation: An HTML tag used to render the title (`"div"`). You can change this parameter's value.

members.name: number
members.documentation: A question number that is calculated based upon the question's [`visibleIndex`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/question#visibleIndex) and survey's [`questionStartIndex`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#questionStartIndex) properties. You can change this parameter's value.

members.name: number
members.documentation: A page number. Note that this is a string value that contains not only the number itself but also the characters that separate the number from the page title: `"1. "`, `"2. "`, etc. You can change this parameter's value.

members.name: number
members.documentation: A panel number. Note that this is a string value that contains not only the number itself but also the characters that separate the number from the panel title: `"1. "`, `"2. "`, etc. You can change this parameter's value.

members.name: questionCount
members.documentation: The total number of questions with input fields. [Image](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/add-image-and-video-to-survey/), [HTML](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-html/), and [Expression](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-expression/) questions are excluded.

members.name: answeredQuestionCount
members.documentation: The number of questions that have been answered.

members.name: requiredQuestionCount
members.documentation: The number of questions marked as required.

members.name: requiredAnsweredQuestionCount
members.documentation: The number of required questions that have been answered.

members.name: text
members.documentation: Progress text rendered in the [progress bar](#showProgressBar). You can change this parameter's value.

members.name: element
members.documentation: A survey element (question, panel, page, or survey) for which the event is raised.

members.name: name
members.documentation: The name of the property that contains the text to process.

members.name: item
members.documentation: A choice item for which the event is raised. This parameter has a value only when `options.element` is a choice-based question, such as [Dropdown](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/dropdown-menu-model) or [Checkboxes](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/checkbox-question-model).

members.name: text
members.documentation: A string with Markdown content. Convert this content to HTML and assign the result to the `options.html` parameter.

members.name: html
members.documentation: A property to which you should assign HTML content.

members.name: renderAs
members.documentation: a component name used for text rendering

members.name: response
members.documentation: A server [response](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XMLHttpRequest/response).

members.name: success
members.documentation: A Boolean value that indicates whether survey results have been saved successfully.

members.name: response
members.documentation: A server [response](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XMLHttpRequest/response).

members.name: success
members.documentation: A Boolean value that indicates whether survey results have been retrieved successfully.

members.name: data
members.documentation:
An object with the following structure:

    ```js
    {
      AnswersCount: number, // A total number of posted answers to the question
      QuestionResult: object // All unique answers to the question and their number
    }
    ```

members.name: dataList
members.documentation:
An array of objects with the following structure:

    ```js
    {
      name: string, // A unique answer to the question
      value: number // The number of user responses with this answer
    }
    ```

members.name: name
members.documentation: A File Upload question's name.

members.name: callback
members.documentation: A callback function that you should call when a file is uploaded successfully or when file upload fails. Pass an array of successfully uploaded files as the first argument. As the second argument, you can pass an array of error messages if file upload failed.

members.name: files
members.documentation: An array of JavaScript File objects that represent files to upload.

members.name: sourceType
members.documentation:
Indicates the origin of the uploaded files.\
Possible values: `"file"`, `"camera"`, or `"signature"`.

members.name: input
members.documentation: A file input HTML element.

members.name: element
members.documentation: A question for which this event is raised.

members.name: elementType
members.documentation:
The type of the element passed as the `options.element` parameter.\
Possible values: any value returned from the [`getType()`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/question#getType) method.

members.name: propertyName
members.documentation: The name of the survey element property for which files are being selected.

members.name: item
members.documentation: A choice item for which the event is raised. This parameter has a value only when the dialog window is opened to select images for an [Image Picker](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/image-picker-question-model) question.

members.name: callback
members.documentation: A callback function to which you should pass selected files.

members.name: callback
members.documentation: A callback function that you should call when a file is downloaded successfully or when deletion fails. Pass `"success"` or `"error"` as the first argument to indicate the operation status. As the second argument, you can pass the downloaded file's data as a Base64 string if file download was successful or an error message if file download failed.

members.name: fileValue
members.documentation: The File Upload question's [`value`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/file-model#value) that contains metadata about uploaded files.

members.name: content
members.documentation: A file identifier (URL, file name, etc.) stored in survey results.

members.name: callback
members.documentation: A callback function that you should call when files are deleted successfully or when deletion fails. Pass `"success"` or `"error"` as the first argument to indicate the operation status. As the second argument, you can pass deleted files' data (`options.value`) if file deletion was successful or an error message if file deletion failed.

members.name: fileName
members.documentation: The name of a file to delete. When this parameter is `null`, all files should be deleted.

If their structure is different, [map their properties](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/map) to bring them to the required structure.
