name: ISaveToJSONOptions
documentation: An interface with configuration options that control how a `SurveyModel` instance is serialized by the [`toJSON()`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#toJSON) method.

members.name: storeDefaults
members.documentation:
Specifies whether the exported JSON schema should include properties whose values are equal to their defaults.

Default value: `false`

members.name: storeLocaleStrings
members.documentation:
Specifies how locale-specific strings are handled during JSON export.

Possible values:

- `true` (default)\
Export the full JSON schema, including all locale strings. Use the [`locales`](#locales) array to restrict the output to specific locales.
- `false`\
Export the JSON schema without any textual content.
- `"stringsOnly"`\
Export a JSON schema that contains only locale strings and the minimal set of properties required to identify survey elements. Use the [`locales`](#locales) array to restrict the output to specific locales. To apply a locale-strings-only schema to a survey model, call the [`mergeLocalizationJSON(json, locales)`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#mergeLocalizationJSON) method.

> As an alternative to calling `toJSON()` with `"stringsOnly"`, you can call the [`getLocalizationJSON(locales)`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#getLocalizationJSON) method, which is syntactic sugar.

members.name: locales
members.documentation: Specifies the locales to include in the exported JSON schema. Applies only when [`storeLocaleStrings`](#storeLocaleStrings) is `true` or `"stringsOnly"`.

name: Serializer
documentation: An alias for the metadata object. It contains object properties' runtime information and allows you to modify it.

name: IExpressionValidationResult
documentation: An interface that describes the result returned by the [`validateExpressions`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#validateExpressions) method.

members.name: obj
members.documentation: A SurveyJS object (question, panel, page, collection item, or the survey itself) that contains the validated expression.

members.name: propertyName
members.documentation: The name of the property that holds the expression.

members.name: errors
members.documentation:
A list of validation errors detected in the expression.

Each error object in this array may contain the following properties:

- `errorType`: `ExpressionErrorType`\
The type of validation error: 0 – `SyntaxError`, 1 – `UnknownFunction`, 2 – `UnknownVariable`, or 3 – `SemanticError`.

- `functionName`: `string`\
The name of the unknown function. Present only when `errorType` is `UnknownFunction`.

- `variableName`: `string`\
The name of the unknown variable. Present only when `errorType` is `UnknownVariable`.

name: Base
documentation: A base class for all SurveyJS objects.

members.name: startCollectDependencies
members.type: (updater: () => void, target: Base, property: string) => void

members.name: isValueEmpty
members.documentation: Returns `true` if a passed `value` is an empty string, array, or object or if it equals to `undefined` or `null`.

parameters.name: value
parameters.documentation: A value to be checked.

parameters.name: trimString
parameters.documentation: *(Optional)* When this parameter is `true`, the method ignores whitespace characters at the beginning and end of a string value. Pass `false` to disable this functionality.

members.name: onPropChangeFunctions
members.type: { name: string; func: (...args: any[]) => void; key: string; }[]

members.name: onPropertyChanged
members.documentation:
An event that is raised when a property of this SurveyJS object has changed.

Parameters:

- `sender`: `this`\
A SurveyJS object whose property has changed.
- `options.name`: `string`\
The name of the changed property.
- `options.newValue`: `any`\
A new value for the property.
- `options.oldValue`: `any`\
An old value of the property. If the property is an array, `oldValue` contains the same array as `newValue` does.

If you need to add and remove property change event handlers dynamically, use the [`registerPropertyChangedHandlers`](#registerPropertyChangedHandlers) and [`unregisterPropertyChangedHandlers`](#unregisterPropertyChangedHandlers) methods instead.

members.name: onItemValuePropertyChanged
members.documentation:
An event that is raised when an [`ItemValue`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/itemvalue) property is changed.

Parameters:

- `sender`: `this`\
A SurveyJS object whose property contains an array of `ItemValue` objects.
- `options.obj`: [`ItemValue`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/itemvalue)\
An `ItemValue` object.
- `options.propertyName`: `string`\
The name of the property to which an array of `ItemValue` objects is assigned (for example, `"choices"` or `"rows"`).
- `options.name`: `"text"` | `"value"`\
The name of the changed property.
- `options.newValue`: `any`\
A new value for the property.
members.type: Event<(sender: Base, options: any) => any, Base, any>

members.name: setPropertyValueCoreHandler
members.type: (propertiesHash: any, name: string, val: any) => void

members.name: getType
members.documentation: Returns the object type as it is used in the JSON schema.

members.name: getOwner
members.documentation: Returns the survey element that owns this element. Returns `undefined` if called on a `SurveyModel` instance.
members.returnDocumentation: The owner survey element, or `undefined` if none exists.

members.name: isPage
members.documentation:
Returns `true` if the survey element is a page.

This property returns `false` for [`PageModel`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/page-model) objects in the following cases:

- `SurveyModel`'s [`questionsOnPageMode`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#questionsOnPageMode) is set to `"singlePage"`.
- The page is included in a [preview of given answers](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey/create-a-multi-page-survey#preview-page).

In those cases, the survey creates an internal `PageModel` object to show all questions on one page, and all regular pages become panels.

members.name: isPanel
members.documentation:
Returns `true` if the survey element is a panel or acts as one.

This property returns `true` for `PageModel` objects in the following cases:

- `SurveyModel`'s [`questionsOnPageMode`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#questionsOnPageMode) is set to `"singlePage"`.
- The page is included in a [preview of given answers](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey/create-a-multi-page-survey#preview-page).

In those cases, the survey creates an internal `PageModel` object to show all questions on one page, and all regular pages become panels.

members.name: isQuestion
members.documentation: Returns `true` if the survey element is a question.

members.name: isSurvey
members.documentation: Returns `true` if the element is a survey.

members.name: isDescendantOf
members.documentation: Use this method to find out if the current object is of a given `typeName` or inherited from it.
members.returnDocumentation: `true` if the current object is of a given `typeName` or inherited from it.

parameters.name: typeName
parameters.documentation: One of the values listed in the [getType()](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/question#getType) description.

members.name: isDesignMode
members.documentation: Returns `true` if the survey is being designed in Survey Creator.

members.name: inSurvey
members.documentation:
Returns `true` if the object is included in a survey.

This property may return `false`, for example, when you [create a survey model dynamically](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey-create-a-simple-survey#create-or-change-a-survey-model-dynamically).

members.name: isLoadingFromJson
members.documentation: Returns `true` if the object configuration is being loaded from JSON.

members.name: toJSON
members.documentation: Returns a JSON schema that corresponds to the current survey element.
members.returnDocumentation: A JSON schema of the survey element.

parameters.name: options
parameters.documentation: An [`ISaveToJSONOptions`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/isavetojsonoptions) object with configuration options.

members.name: getLocalizationJSON
members.documentation:
Returns a JSON schema that contains only locale strings and the minimal set of properties required to identify survey elements.

This method is syntactic sugar for calling the [`toJSON()`](#toJSON) method with the `storeLocaleStrings` option set to `"stringsOnly"`.

To apply a locale-strings-only schema to a survey model, call the [`mergeLocalizationJSON(json, locales)`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#mergeLocalizationJSON) method.
members.returnDocumentation: A locale-strings-only JSON schema.

parameters.name: locales
parameters.documentation: *(Optional)* An array of locale identifiers to include in the JSON schema.

members.name: fromJSON
members.documentation:
Assigns a new JSON schema to the current survey element.

The JSON schema should contain only serializable properties of this survey element. Event handlers and properties that do not belong to the survey element are ignored.

parameters.name: json
parameters.documentation: A JSON schema that you want to apply to the current survey element.

parameters.name: options
parameters.documentation: An object with configuration options.

parameters.name: options.validatePropertyValues
parameters.documentation: Pass `true` if you want to validate property values. Use the [`jsonErrors`](#jsonErrors) array to access validation errors.

members.name: clone
members.documentation: Creates a new object that has the same type and properties as the current SurveyJS object.

members.name: getPropertyByName
members.documentation:
Returns a `JsonObjectProperty` object with metadata about a serializable property that belongs to the current SurveyJS object.

If the property is not found, this method returns `null`.

parameters.name: propName
parameters.documentation: A property name.

members.name: getPropertyValue
members.documentation:
Returns the value of a property with a specified name.

If the property is not found or does not have a value, this method returns either `undefined`, `defaultValue` specified in the property configuration, or a value passed as the `defaultValue` parameter.
members.type: (name: string, defaultValue?: any, calcFunc?: () => any) => any

parameters.name: name
parameters.documentation: A property name.

parameters.name: defaultValue
parameters.documentation: *(Optional)* A value to return if the property is not found or does not have a value.

members.name: setPropertyValue
members.documentation: Assigns a new value to a specified property.

parameters.name: name
parameters.documentation: A property name.

parameters.name: val
parameters.documentation: A new value for the property.

members.name: onPropertyValueChangedCallback
members.type: (name: string, oldValue: any, newValue: any, sender: Base, arrayChanges: ArrayChanges<any>) => void

members.name: itemValuePropertyChanged
members.type: (item: ItemValue, name: string, oldValue: any, newValue: any) => void

members.name: addExpressionProperty
members.type: (name: string, onExecute: (obj: Base, res: any) => void, canRun?: (obj: Base) => boolean, useStrictDependencies?: boolean, onReset?: (obj: Base) => void) => void

members.name: validateExpression
members.type: (name: string, expression: string, options: IExpressionValidationOptions) => IExpressionValidationResult

members.name: validateExpressions
members.returnDocumentation: An [`IExpressionValidationResult`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/IExpressionValidationResult) array.

parameters.name: options
parameters.documentation: Configuration options that control which validation checks are performed.

parameters.name: options.variables
parameters.documentation: Pass `false` to disable validation of unknown variables.

parameters.name: options.functions
parameters.documentation: Pass `false` to disable validation of unknown functions.

parameters.name: options.semantics
parameters.documentation: Pass `false` to disable validation of semantic errors.

members.name: registerPropertyChangedHandlers
members.documentation:
Registers a single value change handler for one or multiple properties.

The `registerPropertyChangedHandlers` and [`unregisterPropertyChangedHandlers`](#unregisterPropertyChangedHandlers) methods allow you to manage property change event handlers dynamically. If you only need to attach an event handler without removing it afterwards, you can use the [`onPropertyChanged`](#onPropertyChanged) event instead.
members.type: (propertyNames: string[], handler: any, key?: string) => void

parameters.name: propertyNames
parameters.documentation: An array of one or multiple property names.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.documentation: A function to call when one of the listed properties change. Accepts a new property value as an argument.

parameters.name: key
parameters.documentation: *(Optional)* A key that identifies the current registration. If a function for one of the properties is already registered with the same key, the function will be overwritten. You can also use the key to subsequently unregister handlers.

members.name: unregisterPropertyChangedHandlers
members.documentation: Unregisters value change event handlers for the specified properties.

parameters.name: propertyNames
parameters.documentation: An array of one or multiple property names.

parameters.name: key
parameters.documentation: *(Optional)* A key of the registration that you want to cancel.

members.name: registerFunctionOnPropertyValueChanged
members.type: (name: string, func: any, key?: string) => void

members.name: registerFunctionOnPropertiesValueChanged
members.type: (names: string[], func: any, key?: string) => void

members.name: onShow
members.documentation: A callback function executed when the dialog is opened.

members.name: onCancel
members.documentation: A callback function executed when users click the Cancel button in the dialog.

members.name: cssClass
members.documentation: A CSS class to apply to the root element of the dialog for custom styling.

members.name: title
members.documentation: The dialog's title.

name: IDialogOptions
documentation:
An interface used to configure the content and behavior of a modal dialog displayed via the [`showDialog()`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/settings#showDialog) method.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/survey-creator/examples/add-modal-property-editor-to-property-grid/ (linkStyle))

members.name: componentName
members.documentation:
The name of the component to render inside the dialog.

This component should be registered in the component collection used by the application (e.g., in `ReactElementFactory` for React and HTML/JS/CSS, `AngularComponentFactory` for Angular, or `app.component()` for Vue.js).

members.name: data
members.documentation: An object with component props.

members.name: onApply
members.documentation:
A callback function executed when users click the Apply button in the dialog.

This function should return `true` to close the dialog or `false` to keep it open (for example, if validation fails).
members.returnDocumentation: `true` to close the dialog or `false` to keep it open.

name: settings
type: { version: string; designMode: { showEmptyDescriptions: boolean; showEmptyTitles: boolean; }; allowShowEmptyDescriptionInDesignMode: boolean; allowShowEmptyTitleInDesignMode: boolean; ... 82 more ...; customFileCategoryName: string; }

name: QuestionMatrixDropdownModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Multi-Select Matrix question type. Multi-Select Matrix allows you to use the [Dropdown](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/questiondropdownmodel), [Checkbox](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/questioncheckboxmodel), [Radiogroup](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/questionradiogroupmodel), [Text](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/questiontextmodel), and [Comment](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/questioncommentmodel) question types as cell editors.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-matrixdropdown/ (linkStyle))

members.name: totalText
members.documentation: A title for the total row. Applies if at least one column displays total values.

members.name: hideIfRowsEmpty
members.documentation: Specifies whether to hide the question when the matrix has no visible rows.

members.name: rowOrder
members.documentation:
Specifies a sort order for matrix rows.

Possible values:

- `"initial"` (default) - Preserves the original order of the `rows` array.
- `"random"` - Arranges matrix rows in random order each time the question is displayed.

name: QuestionDropdownModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Dropdown question type.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-dropdown/ (linkStyle))

members.name: placeholder
members.documentation: A placeholder for the input field.

members.name: selectedItem
members.documentation: Returns the selected choice item. If no item is selected, returns `null`.

members.name: choicesMin
members.documentation:
Use the `choicesMin`, `choicesMax`, and `choicesStep` properties to generate choice items automatically. For example, the configuration below generates three choice items: [10, 20, 30].

```js
"choicesMin": 10,
"choicesMax": 30
"choicesStep": 10
```

members.name: choicesMax
members.documentation:
Use the `choicesMin`, `choicesMax`, and `choicesStep` properties to generate choice items automatically. For example, the configuration below generates three choice items: [10, 20, 30].

```js
"choicesMin": 10,
"choicesMax": 30
"choicesStep": 10
```

members.name: choicesStep
members.documentation:
Use the `choicesMin`, `choicesMax`, and `choicesStep` properties to generate choice items automatically. For example, the configuration below generates three choice items: [10, 20, 30].

```js
"choicesMin": 10,
"choicesMax": 30
"choicesStep": 10
```

The default value of the `choicesStep` property is 1.

members.name: allowClear
members.documentation: Specifies whether to display a button that clears the selected value.

members.name: searchEnabled
members.documentation:
Specifies whether users can enter a value into the input field to filter the drop-down list.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/create-dropdown-menu-in-javascript/ (linkStyle))

members.name: searchMode
members.documentation:
Specifies a comparison operation used to filter the drop-down list. Applies only if [`searchEnabled`](#searchEnabled) is `true`.

Possible values:

- `"contains"` (default)
- `"startsWith"`

members.name: allowCustomChoices
members.documentation:
Specifies whether users can add their own choices if the desired option isn't available in the dropdown.

Default value: `false`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/dropdown-custom-choice-options/ (linkStyle))

> Custom choices will only be stored temporarily for the duration of the current browser session. If you want to save them in a database or another data storage, handle the [`onCreateCustomChoiceItem`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#onCreateCustomChoiceItem) event.

members.name: createCustomChoiceText
members.documentation:
Specifies the text displayed for the command that creates a custom choice. Applies only when [`allowCustomChoices`](#allowCustomChoices) is `true`.

Default value: `"Create \"{0}\" item..."`

members.name: textWrapEnabled
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to wrap long texts in choice options onto a new line.

Default value: `true`

Disable this property if you want the texts to be truncated with ellipsis.

members.name: choicesLazyLoadEnabled
members.documentation: Enables lazy loading. If you set this property to `true`, you should implement the Survey's [`onChoicesLazyLoad`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/surveymodel#onChoicesLazyLoad) event handler.

members.name: choicesLazyLoadPageSize
members.documentation:
Specifies the number of choice items to load at a time when choices are loaded on demand.

Default value: 25

> This property does not accept values below 25 to prevent the server from being flooded with requests.

name: QuestionMatrixModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Single-Select Matrix question type.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/single-selection-matrix-table-question/ (linkStyle))

members.name: cellType
members.documentation:
Specifies the type of matrix cells.

Possible values:

- `"radio"` (default)
- `"checkbox"`

[Radio-Button Matrix Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/single-selection-matrix-table-question/ (linkStyle))

[Checkbox Matrix Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/checkbox-matrix-question/ (linkStyle))

members.name: cellComponent
members.documentation: The name of a component used to render cells.

members.name: eachRowRequired
members.documentation:
Specifies whether each row requires an answer. If a respondent skips a row, the question displays a validation error.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/single-selection-matrix-table-question/ (linkStyle))

members.name: eachRowUnique
members.documentation: Specifies whether answers in all rows should be unique. If any answers duplicate, the question displays a validation error.

members.name: rowOrder
members.documentation:
Specifies a sort order for matrix rows.

Possible values:

- `"initial"` (default) - Preserves the original order of the `rows` array.
- `"random"` - Arranges matrix rows in random order each time the question is displayed.

members.name: hideIfRowsEmpty
members.documentation: Specifies whether to hide the question when the matrix has no visible rows.

members.name: cells
members.documentation:
An array of matrix cells. Use this array to get or set cell values.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-matrix-rubric/ (linkStyle))

members.name: setCellText
members.type: (row: any, column: any, val: string) => void

name: QuestionNonValue
documentation:
A base class for question types that cannot have a value ([Html](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/questionhtmlmodel), [Image](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/questionimagemodel)).

This class does not implement new functionality—it only redefines default values of certain properties inherited from the [`Question`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/question) class.

name: QuestionCheckboxModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Checkboxes question type.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-checkbox/ (linkStyle))

members.name: valuePropertyName
members.documentation:
Specifies a property name used to store selected values.

Set this property if you want to store selected values in an array of objects instead of an array of primitive values. For example, if you set `valuePropertyName` to `"car"`, the `value` property will contain an array of objects `[{ car: "Ford" }, { car: "Tesla" }]`, not an array of string values `[ "Ford", "Tesla" ]`.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/merge-question-values/ (linkStyle))

members.name: selectAllItem
members.documentation: Returns the "Select All" choice item. Use this property to change the item's `value` or `text`.

members.name: selectAllText
members.documentation: Gets or sets a caption for the "Select All" choice item.

members.name: showSelectAllItem
members.documentation: Enable this property to display a "Select All" item. When users select it, all other choice options, except [special options](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/create-checkboxes-question-in-javascript/documentation#display-special-choices), also become selected.

members.name: isAllSelected
members.documentation: Returns `true` if all choice options, except [special options](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/create-checkboxes-question-in-javascript/documentation#display-special-choices), are selected.

members.name: selectAll
members.documentation:
Selects all choice options, except [special options](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/create-checkboxes-question-in-javascript/documentation#display-special-choices).

To clear selection, call the `clearValue()` method.

members.name: maxSelectedChoices
members.documentation:
Specifies the maximum number of selected choices.

Default value: 0 (unlimited)

> This property only limits the number of choice items that can be selected by users. You can select any number of choice items in code, regardless of the `maxSelectedChoices` value.

[Ranking Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/select-items-to-rank/ (linkStyle))

members.name: minSelectedChoices
members.documentation:
Specifies the minimum number of selected choices.

Default value: 0 (unlimited)

> This property only limits the number of choice items that can be selected by users. You can select any number of choice items in code, regardless of the `minSelectedChoices` value.

[Ranking Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/select-items-to-rank/ (linkStyle))

members.name: selectedChoices
members.documentation: An array of selected choice items. Includes the "Other", "None", "Refuse to answer", and "Don't know" choice items if they are selected, but not "Select All". Items are sorted in the order they were selected.

name: QuestionTagboxModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Multi-Select Dropdown (Tag Box) question type.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/how-to-create-multiselect-tag-box/ (linkStyle))

members.name: searchMode
members.documentation:
Specifies a comparison operation used to filter the drop-down list. Applies only if [`searchEnabled`](#searchEnabled) is `true`.

Possible values:

- `"contains"` (default)
- `"startsWith"`

members.name: allowCustomChoices
members.documentation:
Specifies whether users can add their own choices if the desired option isn't available in the dropdown.

Default value: `false`

>  Custom choices will only be stored temporarily for the duration of the current browser session. If you want to save them in a data storage, handle the [`onCreateCustomChoiceItem`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#onCreateCustomChoiceItem) event.

members.name: allowClear
members.documentation: Specifies whether to display a button that clears the selected value.

members.name: searchEnabled
members.documentation: Specifies whether users can enter a value into the input field to filter the drop-down list.

members.name: hideSelectedItems
members.documentation: Specifies whether to remove selected items from the drop-down list.

members.name: choicesLazyLoadEnabled
members.documentation: Enables lazy loading. If you set this property to `true`, you should implement the Survey's [`onChoicesLazyLoad`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/surveymodel#onChoicesLazyLoad) event handler.

members.name: choicesLazyLoadPageSize
members.documentation: Specifies the number of choice items to load at a time when choices are loaded on demand.

members.name: closeOnSelect
members.documentation: Specifies whether to close the drop-down menu after a user selects a value.

members.name: placeholder
members.documentation: A text displayed in the input field when it doesn't have a value.

name: QuestionImagePickerModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Image Picker question type.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/image-picker-question/ (linkStyle))

members.name: multiSelect
members.documentation:
Specifies whether users can select multiple images or videos.

Default value: `false`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/image-picker-question/ (linkStyle))

members.name: showLabel
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to display labels under images or videos. Labels text are taken from the `text` property of each object in the [`choices`](#choices) array.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/image-picker-question/ (linkStyle))

members.name: imageHeight
members.documentation:
Specifies the height of containers for images or videos. Accepts positive numbers and CSS values.

Default value: `auto`

This property allows you to specify the exact image height. If you do not set it, the height will be calculated automatically based on the [`minImageHeight`](#minImageHeight) and [`maxImageHeight`](#maxImageHeight) values and available screen height.

Use the [`imageFit`](#imageFit) property to specify how to fit the images or videos into their containers.

members.name: imageWidth
members.documentation:
Specifies the width of containers for images or videos. Accepts positive numbers and CSS values.

Default value: `auto`

This property allows you to specify the exact image width. If you do not set it, the width will be calculated automatically based on the [`minImageWidth`](#minImageWidth) and [`maxImageWidth`](#maxImageWidth) values and available screen width.

Use the [`imageFit`](#imageFit) property to specify how to fit the images or videos into their containers.

members.name: imageFit
members.documentation:
Specifies how to resize images or videos to fit them into their containers.

Refer to the [`object-fit`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/object-fit) CSS property description for information on accepted values.

members.name: contentMode
members.documentation:
Specifies the type of content that choice items display.

Possible values:

- `"image"` (default) - Images in one of the following formats: JPEG, GIF, PNG, APNG, SVG, BMP, ICO.
- `"video"` - Videos in one of the following formats: MP4, MOV, WMV, FLV, AVI, MKV.

members.name: maxImageWidth
members.documentation:
Specifies a maximum width for image or video containers. Accepts positive numbers and CSS values.

Default value: 3000

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/how-to-create-calculator-form/ (linkStyle))

The `minImageWidth`, `maxImageWidth`, `minImageHeight`, and `maxImageHeight` properties specify boundary values for container sizes. The resulting sizes are selected depending on the available screen space. If you want to specify the exact width and height, use the [`imageWidth`](#imageWidth) and [`imageHeight`](#imageHeight) properties.

members.name: minImageWidth
members.documentation:
Specifies a minimum width for image or video containers. Accepts positive numbers and CSS values.

Default value: 200

The `minImageWidth`, `maxImageWidth`, `minImageHeight`, and `maxImageHeight` properties specify boundary values for container sizes. The resulting sizes are selected depending on the available screen space. If you want to specify the exact width and height, use the [`imageWidth`](#imageWidth) and [`imageHeight`](#imageHeight) properties.

members.name: maxImageHeight
members.documentation:
Specifies a maximum height for image or video containers. Accepts positive numbers and CSS values.

Default value: 3000

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/how-to-create-calculator-form/ (linkStyle))

The `minImageWidth`, `maxImageWidth`, `minImageHeight`, and `maxImageHeight` properties specify boundary values for container sizes. The resulting sizes are selected depending on the available screen space. If you want to specify the exact width and height, use the [`imageWidth`](#imageWidth) and [`imageHeight`](#imageHeight) properties.

members.name: minImageHeight
members.documentation:
Specifies a minimum height for image or video containers. Accepts positive numbers and CSS values.

Default value: 133

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/how-to-create-calculator-form/ (linkStyle))

The `minImageWidth`, `maxImageWidth`, `minImageHeight`, and `maxImageHeight` properties specify boundary values for container sizes. The resulting sizes are selected depending on the available screen space. If you want to specify the exact width and height, use the [`imageWidth`](#imageWidth) and [`imageHeight`](#imageHeight) properties.

name: QuestionRankingModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Ranking question type.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-ranking/ (linkStyle))

members.name: handlePointerDown
members.type: (event: PointerEvent, choice: ItemValue, node: HTMLElement) => void

members.name: handlePointerUp
members.type: (event: PointerEvent, choice: ItemValue, node: HTMLElement) => void

members.name: handleKeydownSelectToRank
members.type: (event: KeyboardEvent, movedElement: ItemValue, hardKey?: string, isNeedFocus?: boolean) => void

members.name: longTap
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to use a long tap (press and hold) gesture to start dragging.

Default value: `true`

Disable this property if you want to start dragging when users perform a scroll gesture.

members.name: selectToRankEnabled
members.documentation:
Specifies whether users can select choices they want to rank.

When you enable this property, the Ranking question displays two areas for ranked and unranked choices. To order choices, users should first drag them from the unranked to the ranked area. Use this mode if you want to let users order only the choices they select.

Default value: `false`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/select-items-to-rank/ (linkStyle))

members.name: selectToRankAreasLayout
members.documentation:
Specifies the layout of the ranked and unranked areas. Applies when [`selectToRankEnabled`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/ranking-question-model#selectToRankEnabled) is `true`.

Possible values:

- `"horizontal"` (default) - The ranked and unranked areas are positioned next to each other. Users drag and drop choices between them in the horizontal direction.
- `"vertical"`- The ranked area is positioned above the unranked area. Users drag and drop choices between them in the vertical direction.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/select-items-to-rank/ (linkStyle))

members.name: selectToRankEmptyRankedAreaText
members.documentation: A placeholder displayed in the area for ranked choices. Applies when [`selectToRankEnabled`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/ranking-question-model#selectToRankEnabled) is `true`.

members.name: selectToRankEmptyUnrankedAreaText
members.documentation: A placeholder displayed in the area for unranked choices. Applies when [`selectToRankEnabled`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/ranking-question-model#selectToRankEnabled) is `true`.

name: QuestionCommentModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Long Text question type.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-comment/ (linkStyle))

members.name: rows
members.documentation:
Specifies the visible height of the comment area, measured in lines.

The value of this property is passed on to the `rows` attribute of the underlying `<textarea>` element.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/add-open-ended-question-to-a-form/ (linkStyle))

members.name: acceptCarriageReturn
members.documentation:
Specifies whether the question allows line breaks.

When this property is enabled, a user can press Enter to insert line breaks. They are saved as `\n` in survey results. The Comment question also recognizes and interprets the `\n` sequence as a line break when you set the question `value` in code.

members.name: autoGrow
members.documentation:
Specifies whether the comment area automatically increases its height to accomodate multi-line content.

Default value: `false` (inherited from `SurveyModel`'s [`autoGrowComment`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/surveymodel#autoGrowComment) property)

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/add-open-ended-question-to-a-form/ (linkStyle))

members.name: allowResize
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to display a resize handle for the comment area.

Default value: `true` (inherited from `SurveyModel`'s [`allowResizeComment`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/surveymodel#allowResizeComment) property)

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/add-open-ended-question-to-a-form/ (linkStyle))

name: QuestionHtmlModel
documentation:
A class that describes the HTML question type. Unlike other question types, HTML cannot have a title or value.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-html/ (linkStyle))

members.name: html
members.documentation:
HTML markup to display.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/add-html-form-field/ (linkStyle))

> If you get the markup from a third party, ensure that it does not contain malicious code.

name: QuestionRadiogroupModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Radio Button Group question type.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-radiogroup/ (linkStyle))

members.name: selectedItem
members.documentation: Returns the selected choice item. If no item is selected, returns `null`.

members.name: allowClear
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to display a button that clears the question value.

Default value: `false`

name: QuestionRatingModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Rating Scale question type.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/rating-scale/ (linkStyle))

members.name: rateValues
members.documentation:
A list of rate values.

This property accepts an array of objects with the following structure:

```js
{
  "value": any, // A value to be saved in survey results
  "text": string, // A display text. This property supports Markdown. When `text` is undefined, `value` is used.
  "customProperty": any // Any property that you find useful.
}
```

If you add custom properties, refer to the following help topic to learn how to serialize them into JSON: [Add Custom Properties to Property Grid](https://surveyjs.io/survey-creator/documentation/property-grid#add-custom-properties-to-the-property-grid).

To enable Markdown support for the `text` property, implement Markdown-to-HTML conversion in the [onTextMarkdown](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#onTextMarkdown) event handler. For an example, refer to the following demo: [Convert Markdown to HTML with markdown-it](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/edit-survey-questions-markdown/).

If you need to specify only the `value` property, you can set the `rateValues` property to an array of numbers, for example, `[ 3, 6, 10 ]`. These values are both saved in survey results and used as display text.

If you do not specify the `rateValues` property, rate values are generated automatically based upon the [`rateMin`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateMin), [`rateMax`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateMax), [`rateStep`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateStep), and [`rateCount`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateCount) property values.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/rating-scale/ (linkStyle))

members.name: rateMin
members.documentation:
Specifies the first rate value in the generated sequence of rate values. Applies if the [`rateValues`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateValues) array is empty.

Default value: 1

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/rating-scale/ (linkStyle))

members.name: rateMax
members.documentation:
Specifies the last rate value in the generated sequence of rate values. Applies if the [`rateValues`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateValues) array is empty.

Default value: 5

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/rating-scale/ (linkStyle))

members.name: rateStep
members.documentation:
Specifies a step with which to generate rate values. Applies if the [`rateValues`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateValues) array is empty.

Default value: 1

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/rating-scale/ (linkStyle))

members.name: rateCount
members.documentation:
Specifies the number of rate values you want to generate. Applies if the [`rateValues`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateValues) array is empty.

Set the [`rateMin`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateMin) or [`rateMax`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateMax) property to specify the first or the last rate value. Use the [`rateStep`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateStep) property to specify a step with which to generate rate values.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/rating-scale/ (linkStyle))

members.name: itemValuePropertyChanged
members.type: (item: ItemValue, name: string, oldValue: any, newValue: any) => void

members.name: minRateDescription
members.documentation: Specifies a description for the minimum (first) rate value.

members.name: maxRateDescription
members.documentation: Specifies a description for the maximum (last) rate value.

members.name: displayRateDescriptionsAsExtremeItems
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to display [`minRateDescription`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#minRateDescription) and [`maxRateDescription`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#maxRateDescription) values as captions for buttons that correspond to the extreme (first and last) rate values.

Default value: `false`

If this property is disabled, the `minRateDescription` and `maxRateDescription` values are displayed as plain non-clickable texts.

If any of the `minRateDescription` and `maxRateDescription` properties is empty, the corresponding rate value's `value` or `text` is displayed as a button caption.

members.name: displayMode
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to display rate values as buttons or items in a drop-down list.

Possible values:

- `"buttons"` - Displays rate values as buttons in a row.
- `"dropdown"` - Displays rate values as items in a drop-down list.
- `"auto"` (default) - Selects between the `"buttons"` and `"dropdown"` modes based on the available width. When the width is insufficient to display buttons, the question displays a dropdown.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/ui-adaptation-modes-for-rating-scale/ (linkStyle))

members.name: rateDescriptionLocation
members.documentation:
Specifies the alignment of [`minRateDescription`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#minRateDescription) and [`maxRateDescription`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#maxRateDescription) texts.

Possible values:

- `"leftRight"` (default) - Aligns `minRateDescription` to the left of rate values and `maxRateDescription` to their right.
- `"top"` - Displays the descriptions above the minimum and maximum rate values.
- `"bottom"` - Displays both descriptions below the minimum and maximum rate values.
- `"topBottom"` - Displays `minRateDescription` above the minimum rate value and `maxRateDescription` below the maximum rate value.

members.name: rateType
members.documentation:
Specifies the visual representation of rate values.

Possible values:

- `"labels"` (default) - Displays rate values as buttons with labels.
- `"stars"` - Displays rate values as stars.
- `"smileys"` - Displays rate values as smiley faces.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/rating-scale/ (linkStyle))

members.name: scaleColorMode
members.documentation:
Specifies how to colorize the smiley face rating scale. Applies only if [`rateType`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateType) is `"smileys"`.

Possible values:

- `"monochrome"` (default) - Displays emojis in monochrome.
- `"colored"` - Displays emojis in color.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/rating-scale/ (linkStyle))

members.name: rateColorMode
members.documentation:
Specifies how to colorize the selected emoji. Applies only if [`rateType`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/rating-scale-question-model#rateType) is `"smileys"`.

Possible values:

- `"default"` - Displays the selected emoji in default survey color.
- `"scale"` (default) - Inherits the color from the scale.

members.name: itemComponent
members.documentation: The name of a component used to render items.

members.name: getItemStyle
members.returnType: { "--sd-rating-item-color"?: undefined; "--sd-rating-item-color-light"?: undefined; } | { "--sd-rating-item-color": string; "--sd-rating-item-color-light": string; } | { "--sd-rating-item-color": string; "--sd-rating-item-color-light"?: undefined; }

name: QuestionSliderModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Slider question type.

[View Slider Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/single-value-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

[View Range Slider Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/dual-range-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

members.name: sliderType
members.documentation:
Specifies whether the slider allows selecting a single value (`"single"`) or a value range (`"range"`).

Possible values:

- `"single"` (default)
- `"range"`

[View Slider Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/single-value-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

[View Range Slider Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/dual-range-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

members.name: max
members.documentation:
Defines the maximum value on the slider scale.

Default value: 100

[View Slider Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/single-value-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

[View Range Slider Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/dual-range-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

members.name: min
members.documentation:
Defines the minimum value on the slider scale.

Default value: 0

[View Slider Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/single-value-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

[View Range Slider Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/dual-range-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

members.name: maxValueExpression
members.documentation:
An expression that dynamically calculates the maximum scale value. Overrides the static [`max`](#max) property if defined.

[Expressions](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey/conditional-logic#expressions (linkStyle))

members.name: minValueExpression
members.documentation:
An expression that dynamically calculates the minimum scale value. Overrides the static [`min`](#min) property if defined.

[Expressions](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/design-survey/conditional-logic#expressions (linkStyle))

members.name: maxRangeLength
members.documentation:
Specifies the maximum length between the two thumbs of a range slider. Applies only if [`sliderType`](#sliderType) is `"range"`.

Default value: `null`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/dual-range-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

members.name: minRangeLength
members.documentation:
Specifies the minimum length between the two thumbs of a range slider. Applies only if [`sliderType`](#sliderType) is `"range"`.

Default value: `null`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/dual-range-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

members.name: tooltipFormat
members.documentation:
A formatting string for thumb tooltips. You can use `{0}` as a placeholder for a tooltip's numeric value.

Default value: `"{0}"`

members.name: labelFormat
members.documentation:
A formatting string for [auto-generated](#labelCount) or [custom labels](#customLabels). You can use `{0}` as a placeholder for the label's numeric value.

Default value: `"{0}"`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/customize-slider-scale-labels/ (linkStyle))

> If you are using custom labels, `labelFormat` affects only those that do not define the `text` property.

members.name: tooltipVisibility
members.documentation:
Controls the visibility of thumb tooltips.

Possible values:

- `"auto"` (default) - Tooltips appear when the thumb or selected range is hovered or focused.
- `"always"`- Tooltips are always visible.
- `"never"` - Tooltips are never displayed.

members.name: step
members.documentation:
Sets the interval between selectable scale values.

Default value: 1

[View Slider Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/single-value-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

[View Range Slider Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/dual-range-slider-input/ (linkStyle))

members.name: showLabels
members.documentation:
Specifies whether the slider displays value labels along the scale.

Default value: `true`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/customize-slider-scale-labels/ (linkStyle))

members.name: labelCount
members.documentation:
Defines how many auto-generated labels should be displayed along the slider scale. Ignored if the [`customLabels`](#customLabels) property is set.

Default value: -1 (the number of labels is calculated automatically based on the [`min`](#min) and [`max`](#max) values)

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/customize-slider-scale-labels/ (linkStyle))

members.name: customLabels
allowSwap
members.documentation:
Allows the start and end thumbs to cross over each other. If `false`, the thumbs cannot be swapped. Applies only if [`sliderType`](#sliderType) is `"range"`.

Default value: `false` if [`minRangeLength`](#minRangeLength) is defined, `true` otherwise.

members.name: allowClear
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to display a button that clears the selected slider value and resets it to `undefined`.

Default value: `false`

members.name: itemValuePropertyChanged
members.type: (item: ItemValue, name: string, oldValue: any, newValue: any) => void

name: QuestionBooleanModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Yes/No (Boolean) question type.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-boolean/ (linkStyle))

members.name: booleanValue
members.documentation:
Gets or sets the question value as a Boolean value.

If you set the `valueTrue` and `valueFalse` properties, the `value` property contains their values instead of Boolean values. This may be inconvenient when you operate the question value in code. To access the standard Boolean values, use the `booleanValue` property.

members.name: labelTrue
members.documentation:
Gets or sets a text label that corresponds to a positive answer.

Default value: "Yes"

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/yes-no-question/ (linkStyle))

members.name: swapOrder
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to swap the order of the Yes and No answers.

Default value: `false`

By default, the order is [ "No", "Yes"]. Enable this property to reorder the answers as follows: [ "Yes", "No" ].

members.name: labelFalse
members.documentation:
Gets or sets a text label that corresponds to a negative answer.

Default value: "No"

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/yes-no-question/ (linkStyle))

members.name: valueTrue
members.documentation:
A value to save in survey results when respondents give a positive answer.

Default value: `true`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/yes-no-question/ (linkStyle))

members.name: valueFalse
members.documentation:
A value to save in survey results when respondents give a negative answer.

Default value: `false`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/yes-no-question/ (linkStyle))

name: QuestionImageModel
documentation:
A class that describes the Image question type. Unlike other question types, Image cannot have a title or value.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/questiontype-image/ (linkStyle))

members.name: imageLink
members.documentation:
Specifies an image or video URL.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/add-image-and-video-to-survey/ (linkStyle))

members.name: altText
members.documentation: Specifies a value for the `alt` attribute of the underlying `` element. [View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/add-image-and-video-to-survey/ (linkStyle))

members.name: imageHeight
members.documentation:
Specifies the height of a container for the image or video. Accepts positive numbers and CSS values.

Default value: 150

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/add-image-and-video-to-survey/ (linkStyle))

> Use the [`imageFit`](#imageFit) property to specify how to fit the image or video into the container.

members.name: imageWidth
members.documentation:
Specifies the width of a container for the image or video. Accepts positive numbers and CSS values.

Default value: 200

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/add-image-and-video-to-survey/ (linkStyle))

> Use the [`imageFit`](#imageFit) property to specify how to fit the image or video into the container.

members.name: imageFit
members.documentation:
Specifies how to resize the image or video to fit it into its container.

Refer to the [`object-fit`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/object-fit) CSS property description for information on accepted values.

members.name: contentMode
members.documentation:
Specifies the type of content that the Image question displays.

Possible values:

- `"image"` - An image in one of the following formats: JPEG, GIF, PNG, APNG, SVG, BMP, ICO.
- `"video"` - A video in one of the following formats: MP4, MOV, WMV, FLV, AVI, MKV.
- `"youtube"` - A link to a YouTube video.
- `"auto"` (default) - Selects one of the above based on the [`imageLink`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/questionimagemodel#imageLink) property.

members.name: renderedMode
members.documentation: Returns the type of content that the Image question displays: `"image"`, `"video"`, or `"youtube"`.

name: PopupSurveyModel
documentation:
A class that renders a survey in a pop-up window.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/popup-survey/ (linkStyle))

members.name: survey
members.documentation: A [`SurveyModel`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model) instance rendered in the pop-up window.

members.name: closeOnCompleteTimeout
members.documentation:
Specifies how many seconds the pop-up window should remain open after users complete the survey.

Default value: 0 (the window is closed immediately)

Set this property to a negative value (for instance, -1) to keep the pop-up window open without a time limit.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/popup-survey/ (linkStyle))

members.name: isShowing
members.documentation:
Indicates whether the pop-up survey appears on the page, regardless of its [expand state](#isExpanded).

You can set this property to `true` or `false` to control visibility of the pop-up survey. Alternatively, you can use the [`show()`](#show) and [`hide()`](#hide) methods.

members.name: show
members.documentation:
Shows the pop-up survey. The survey may appear [expanded or collapsed](#isExpanded).

As an alternative to this method, you can set the [`isShowing`](#isShowing) property to `true`.

members.name: hide
members.documentation:
Hides the pop-up survey.

As an alternative to this method, you can set the [`isShowing`](#isShowing) property to `false`.

members.name: isExpanded
members.documentation:
Indicates whether the pop-up window is expanded or collapsed.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/popup-survey/ (linkStyle))

You can set this property to `true` or `false` to control the expand state of the pop-up survey. Alternatively, you can use the [`expand()`](#expand) and [`collapse()`](#collapse) methods.

members.name: title
members.documentation: A title for the pop-up window. If this property is undefined, the title is taken from [`SurveyModel`](#survey)'s [`title`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/survey-data-model#title) property.

members.name: expand
members.documentation:
Expands the pop-up window.

As an alternative to this method, you can set the [`isExpanded`](#isExpanded) property to `true`.

members.name: collapse
members.documentation:
Collapses the pop-up window, leaving only the survey title visible.

As an alternative to this method, you can set the [`isExpanded`](#isExpanded) property to `false`.

members.name: allowClose
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to display a button that closes the pop-up window.

Default value: `false`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/popup-survey/ (linkStyle))

If you allow users to close the pop-up window, make sure to implement a UI element that opens it. This element should call the [`show()`](#show) method or enable the [`isShowing`](#isShowing) property.

members.name: allowFullScreen
members.documentation:
Specifies whether to display a button that allows respondents to show the pop-up survey in full screen mode.

Default value: `false`

name: InputMaskPattern

members.name: pattern
members.documentation:
A pattern for the input value.

If you set the [`maskType`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/text-entry-question-model#maskType) to `"pattern"`, the mask can contain string literals and the following placeholders:

- `9` - A digit.
- `a` - An upper- or lower-case letter.
- `#` - A digit or an upper- or lower-case letter.

Characters not listed above are treated as literals automatically. Use backslash `\` to escape a definition character and insert it as a literal (e.g., `\9` inserts a literal `9`).

Example: `+1(999)-999-99-99`

If you set the [`maskType`](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/documentation/api-reference/text-entry-question-model#maskType) to `"datetime"`, the mask can contain separator characters and the following placeholders:

- `m` - Month number.
- `mm` - Month number, with leading zero for single-digit values.
- `d` - Day of the month.
- `dd` - Day of the month, with leading zero for single-digit values.
- `yy` - Last two digits of the year.
- `yyyy` - A four-digit year.
- `H` - Hours in 24-hour format.
- `HH` - Hours in 24-hour format, with leading zero for single-digit values.
- `h` - Hours in 12-hour format.
- `hh` - Hours in 12-hour format, with leading zero for single-digit values.
- `MM` - Minutes.
- `ss` - Seconds.
- `TT` - 12-hour clock period in upper case (AM/PM).
- `tt` - 12-hour clock period in lower case (am/pm).

Example: `mm/dd/yyyy HH:MM:ss`

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/masked-input-fields/ (linkStyle))

name: InputMaskNumeric

members.name: allowNegativeValues
members.documentation:
Specifies whether respondents can enter negative values.

Default value: `true`

members.name: decimalSeparator
members.documentation:
A symbol used to separate the fractional part from the integer part of a displayed number.

Default value: `"."`

members.name: precision
members.documentation:
Limits how many digits to retain after the decimal point for a displayed number.

Default value: 2

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/masked-input-fields/ (linkStyle))

members.name: thousandsSeparator
members.documentation:
A symbol used to separate the digits of a large number into groups of three.

Default value: `","`

members.name: min
members.documentation: A minimum value that respondents can enter.

members.name: max
members.documentation: A maximum value that respondents can enter.

members.name: displayNumber
members.type: (parsedNumber: INumericalComposition, insertThousandsSeparator?: boolean, matchWholeMask?: boolean) => string

name: InputMaskDateTime

members.name: min
members.documentation: A minimum date and time value that respondents can enter.

members.name: max
members.documentation: A maximum date and time value that respondents can enter.

name: InputMaskCurrency

members.name: prefix
members.documentation:
One or several symbols to be displayed before the currency value.

[View Demo](https://surveyjs.io/form-library/examples/masked-input-fields/ (linkStyle))

members.name: suffix
members.documentation: One or several symbols to be displayed after the currency value.
